Root cause VERIFIED at source: sg_gate_checks (bin/lib/dmc-release-gate.py:393-394) builds G2's allowlist from ALL scope.lock files[].path; the v0.2.6 runner G2 (bin/lib/dmc-v0.2.6-gate-check-runner.sh:47-50) FAILs unless every allowlist path is staged. The two never-firing regenerate-IF-drift rows (AGENTS.md, INSTALL_MANIFEST.md) were unsatisfiable staging obligations -> the observed G2 FAIL approved files not staged. My r1 endorsement of those rows treated lock rows as authorizations, not obligations; the deterministic gate correctly caught the gap (C11 advisory-critic + deterministic-gate defense-in-depth working as designed). The Rev 3 fix (conditional-edit authorization OUT of the lock; drift => HALT + follow-up scope) makes the lock == exactly the 3 changed paths, so G1 (staged subset of allowlist) and G2 (allowlist fully staged) both pass.

Confirmed the change set is EXACTLY the 6 disclosed edits plus the 5-site consistency sweep, nothing else. (1) Relevant Files AGENTS.md/INSTALL_MANIFEST.md rows -> Allowed-to-Edit: no (lines 117-118); (2) contingency Proposed Change -> NOT-an-in-lock-edit, Files none-in-this-lock (178-185); (3) AC criterion 7 -> scope.lock == exactly 3 paths AND staged == same 3 (216-221); (4) new Risk row G2-staging-obligation with the two source refs + B/C changed-files-only norm (232); (5) Notes Rev 3 line (302). Goal, User Intent, F1-F4, F6, F7 body, Out of Scope, Proposed Changes 1-3, AC criteria 1-4/6, Assumptions, T001, T003, and the entire Verification Commands table are byte-consistent with the r2-reviewed draft.

The sweep did NOT weaken any AC or verification obligation. The neutrality diffs (agents-md --stdout diff AGENTS.md EMPTY; --emit-manifest diff INSTALL_MANIFEST.md EMPTY) remain Required=yes in the Verification Commands table (lines 282-283), remain the Verification Method of AC criterion 5 (208-209), and are still executed in T002 (260-262). Only the failure branch changed: regenerate-in-lockstep -> HALT + open a follow-up scope. Detection of drift is fully preserved (verification still fails the cycle on drift); only the RESPONSE is now escalate-not-edit, which is stricter. The scope+gate criterion tightened (exact 3-path lock AND staged == same 3).

Security/correctness lens. drift => HALT + follow-up scope does NOT reintroduce the one-sided-lockstep forbidden fix class my r1 guarded against; it is a STRICTER form of the same discipline. It never hand-edits the check (masking explicitly forbidden, lines 184-185), never makes a one-sided in-lock edit; a genuine regeneration (if ever needed) happens under a fresh authorized + gated scope where the artifact and any lockstep pair move together, preserving lockstep integrity — the plan never ships a half-regenerated state. F5 (live-verified NO-OP, both diffs EMPTY at bbeb277 incl. the suite --root <abs> form) makes the branch defensive-only; the committed change is byte-neutral to both artifacts.

Scope lens. Rev 3 REMOVES two paths from the editable scope (5 -> 3); a strict subset of an already-authorized scope requires no new human authorization because narrowing can only reduce blast radius, never expand it. The human envelope gate (user directive 2026-07-10) authorized up to the 5-path scope; using exactly 3 is within that grant on the same directive basis. No protected surface added; bin/dmc remains the sole enforcement-class landmark (non-degrading FLAG expected, no G4 override).

Internally consistent: Relevant Files (117-118), contingency change (178-185), AC criterion 7 (216-221), Risks G2 row (232), F5 tail (90-92), and Notes Rev 3 (302) all agree on the HALT posture and the exact 3-path lock.
